committee_reports.title: MILITARY CONSTRUCTION, VETERANS AFFAIRS, AND RELATED AGENCIES APPROPRIATIONS BILL, 2025

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 46 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to prohibit the use of funds for the Department of Veterans Affairs to process medical claims for the Immigration and Customs Enforcement Health Service Corps.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Steube amendment (A031) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 45 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to prohibit the Department of Veterans Affairs from using its funds to pay bonuses to senior executives in the VA Central Office.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Steube amendment (A030) Agreed to by recorded vote: 237 - 169, 1 Present (Roll no. 245).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 44 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding by $10 million to conduct a study on the benefits of utilizing artificial intelligence (AI) to streamline oversight, mitigate and reduce fraud, and improve data accuracy and financial management practices at the Department of Veterans Affairs.
key_amendments.purpose: Amendment increases and decreases funding by $10 million to conduct a study on the benefits of utilizing artificial intelligence (AI) to streamline oversight, mitigate and reduce fraud, and improve data accuracy and financial management practices at the Department of Veterans Affairs.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Schweikert amendment (A029) Agreed to by recorded vote: 392 - 11 (Roll no. 244).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 43 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding by $1 million for the Veterans Benefits Administration to provide support for utilizing AI to expedite claims.
key_amendments.purpose: Amendment increases and decreases funding by $1 million for the Veterans Benefits Administration to provide support for utilizing artificial intelligence to expedite claims.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Schweikert amendment (A028) Agreed to by recorded vote: 333 - 70 (Roll no. 243).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 42 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to prohibit funds to modify or remove any display of the Department of Veterans Affairs that bears the mission statement "To fulfill President Lincoln's promise 'to care for him who shall have borne the battle, and for his widow, and his orphan' by serving and honoring the men and women who are America's veterans.".

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Self amendment (A027) Agreed to by recorded vote: 206 - 200 (Roll no. 241).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 41 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding for the Family Housing Operation and Maintenance, Air Force, account to emphasize the importance of housing oversight.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Rodgers (WA) amendment (A026) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 40 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding for the Medical Community Care account to express the need for the Department of Veterans Affairs to address emergency care coverage.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Rodgers (WA) amendment (A025) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 39 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding to direct the Department of Veteran Affairs to collaborate with the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine to conduct a comprehensive study determining and identifying exposures associated with being a military aviator, connections between these exposures and various cancers, and prevalence of and mortality from specified cancers among those who served as active-duty aircrew.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Pfluger amendment (A024) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 38 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease appropriations for the Veterans Health Administration - Medical Services - Supportive Services for Low Income Veterans and Families (SSVF) program by $1,000,000 to recognize the importance of SSVF's Shallow Subsidy program in keeping veterans in their homes and preventing veterans from falling into homelessness.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Peters amendment (A023) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 37 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease appropriations for the Veterans Health Administration - Medical Services - HUD-VA Supportive Housing (HUD-VASH) program by $1,000,000 to encourage local VA systems and public housing authorities to work together to streamline the voucher application process to reduce barriers for veterans seeking housing assistance.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Peters amendment (A022) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 36 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease Veterans Health Administration, Medical Services, by $1 million for furnishing Stellate Ganglion Block (SGB) therapy to personnel who elect to receive the treatment.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Perry amendment (A021) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 35 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding for grants for construction of State Extended Care Facilities to focus on creating or maintaining nursing homes for Americas World War II, Korean, and Vietnam Veterans.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Ogles amendment (A020) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 34 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding for the Board of Veterans Appeals to prioritize increasing processing speed for veteran casework.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Ogles amendment (A019) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 33 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding for the Veterans Health Administration's medical community care account to increase accessibility for in-home health care services for veterans.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Ogles amendment (A018) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 32 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding for medical services at the Veterans Health Administration for the treatment of post-traumatic stress disorder in veterans.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Ogles amendment (A017) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 31 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding for the Veterans Housing Benefit Program Fund for the development of improved informational materials regarding the relative benefits of a VA home loan versus other types of housing loans for different homebuyer profiles.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Ogles amendment (A016) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 29 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease the Veterans Health Administration (VHA) account by $10 million to reinforce the importance of strengthening the VHA's Office of Mental Health and its work to assist veterans with disabilities and mental health and substance abuse challenges.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Molinaro amendment (A015) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 28 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to ensure veterans can participate in state-approved medical marijuana programs. The amendment removes the current directive that prevents VA Medical Facility staff from recommending, making referrals, or completing forms and registering Veterans for participation in state-approved marijuana programs.
key_amendments.purpose: Amendment allows veterans to participate in state-approved medical marijuana programs and removes the current directive that prevents the Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Facility staff from recommending, making referrals, or completing forms and registering Veterans for participation in state-approved marijuana programs.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Mast amendment (A014) Agreed to by recorded vote: 290 - 116 (Roll no. 240).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 22 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding for the purpose of expressing the urgency of expeditiously and efficiently utilizing increased demolition funding on Navy and Marine Corps bases.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Kiggans (VA) amendment (A013) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 21 printed in Part B of House Report 118-535 to increase and decrease funding for the purpose of ensuring that the Department of Veterans Affairs fully funds and competently executes mental health care programs, with a special emphasis on suicide prevention and associated outreach.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Kiggans (VA) amendment (A012) Agreed to by voice vote.

committee_reports.title: DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY APPROPRIATIONS BILL, 2025

hr8771.title: Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2025

latest_action.text: Read twice. Placed on Senate Legislative Calendar under General Orders. Calendar No. 507.

committee_reports.title: STATE, FOREIGN OPERATIONS, AND RELATED PROGRAMS APPROPRIATIONS BILL, 2025

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 75 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to clarify the funding prohibition of taxpayer dollars going to the Assad regime in the bill encompasses taxpayer dollars that are provided both directly and indirectly to the regime.
key_amendments.purpose: Amendment clarifies that no foreign assistance funds in the bill may be made available to areas of Syria controlled by Bashar al-Assad, both directly and indirectly.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Wilson (SC) amendment (A038) Agreed to by recorded vote: 257 - 154 (Roll no. 322).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 72 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funds from this Act from being used to support the Gaza pier.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Waltz amendment (A037) Agreed to by recorded vote: 209 - 200 (Roll no. 321).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 68 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funds from being used to pay any United States contribution to the United Nations or any affiliated agency of the United Nations.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Tiffany amendment (A036) Failed by recorded vote: 149 - 259 (Roll no. 320).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 67 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 Prohibits funds from this Act from being used to enforce arbitrary restrictions on executive branch officials regarding travel to Taiwan and normal communication with Taiwanese officials imposed by the State Department through the "Memorandum for All Department and Agency Executive Secretaries" entitled "Revised Guidelines on Interaction with Taiwan" dated June 29, 2021.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Tiffany amendment (A035) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 64 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit federal funds to pay the salary of, reinstate, or reemploy Robert Malley.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Tenney amendment (A034) Agreed to by recorded vote: 218 - 188 (Roll no. 319).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 63 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funding for Executive Order 14019, relating to Promoting Access to Voting.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Tenney amendment (A033) Agreed to by recorded vote: 208 - 202 (Roll no. 318).

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 62 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funds from being delivered to the government of Iraq.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Steube amendment (A032) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 61 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funds from being provided to the Lebanese Armed Forces.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Steube amendment (A031) Failed by recorded vote: 103 - 308 (Roll no. 317). (consideration: CR H4347-4348)

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 59 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funding for the United Nations unless specifically authorized by Congress.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Spartz amendment (A030) Failed by recorded vote: 182 - 230 (Roll no. 316). (consideration: CR H4347)

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 57 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to strike paragraph prohibiting support of Migration Management.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Ramirez amendment (A029) Failed by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 54 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to strike International Disaster Assistance funding.
key_amendments.purpose: Amendment sought to reduce the account for International Disaster Assistance by $3,452,362,000 and apply the savings to the Spending Reduction Account.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Perry amendment (A028) Failed by recorded vote: 101 - 312 (Roll no. 315). (consideration: CR H4346-4347)

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 50 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit the use of funds from being used to administer or enforce National Security Memorandum 20 (National Security Memorandum on Safeguards and Accountability With Respect to Transferred Defense Articles and Defense Services).
key_amendments.purpose: Amendment prohibits the use of funds from being used to administer or enforce National Security Memorandum 20 (National Security Memorandum on Safeguards and Accountability With Respect to Transferred Defense Articles and Defense Services).

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Ogles amendment (A027) Agreed to by recorded vote: 216 - 197 (Roll no. 314). (consideration: CR H4345-4346)

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 49 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit the use of funds from being made available to pay the salary and expenses of Antony Blinken.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Ogles amendment (A026) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 48 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit the use of funds to delist the People's Republic of China from the "Majors List".

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Ogles amendment (A025) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 45 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funds appropriated by this act from being made available for the State Department to cite statistics obtained from the Gaza Health Ministry.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Moskowitz amendment (A024) Agreed to by recorded vote: 269 - 144 (Roll no. 313). (consideration: CR H4345)

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 36 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to reduce Migration and Refugee Assistance funding by $500 million and increase International Narcotics Control and Law Enforcement funding by $500 million.
key_amendments.purpose: Amendment reduces Migration and Refugee Assistance funding by $500 million and increases International Narcotics Control and Law Enforcement funding by a similar amount.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Mace amendment (A023) Agreed to by recorded vote: 213 - 199 (Roll no. 312). (consideration: CR H4344-4345)

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 35 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funds made available by this Act from being obligated, expended, or otherwise made available to the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Mace amendment (A022) Failed by recorded vote: 163 - 249 (Roll no. 311). (consideration: CR H4343-4344)

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 32 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to increase funding for the Peace Corps account by $20 million to match the FY24 enacted level and to decrease funding for the Office of the Secretary of State, through the Diplomatic Programs account, by $20 million.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Lawler amendment (A021) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 24 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funds appropriated by this Act from being made available to support the implementation or negotiations toward an extension of the Scientific and Technological Cooperation Protocol Between the United States of America and China.

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Issa amendment (A020) Agreed to by voice vote.

key_amendments.description: An amendment numbered 23 printed in Part B of House Report 118-559 to prohibit funding for documents, statements, or other communications that refers to the General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party by the title of "President".

latest_action.text: On agreeing to the Issa amendment (A019) Agreed to by voice vote.
